differential_diagnoses: Pyoderma Gangrenosum (PG) with systemic involvement (neutrophilic disease)
PASH syndrome (Pyoderma gangrenosum, Acne, and Hidradenitis suppurativa)
SAPHO syndrome (Synovitis, Acne, Pustulosis, Hyperostosis, and Osteitis)
next_steps: Perform a punch biopsy of the advancing edge of an active skin ulcer for histopathology and tissue culture to rule out infection and confirm neutrophilic infiltrate
Initiate high-dose systemic corticosteroids (e.g., intravenous methylprednisolone) as the primary treatment for suspected pyoderma gangrenosum
Measure serum inflammatory markers (CRP and ESR) and fecal calprotectin
Perform colonoscopy and/or capsule endoscopy to evaluate for asymptomatic or subclinical Crohn's disease
Perform a liver biopsy of the peripheral lesions to evaluate for sterile neutrophilic abscesses
Consult rheumatology and dermatology for multi-disciplinary management of systemic autoinflammatory process
Avoid unnecessary surgical debridement of wounds to prevent worsening of pathergy
